SEARCH
Module qupath.fx
Package qupath.fx.charts

Class TransformedAxis

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.chart.Axis<Number>
javafx.scene.chart.ValueAxis<Number>
qupath.fx.charts.TransformedAxis
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget
public abstract class TransformedAxis extends javafx.scene.chart.ValueAxis<Number>
A class providing a minimal implementation of a transformed axis.

    • calculateMinorTickMarks

      protected abstract List<Number> calculateMinorTickMarks()
      Calculate the minor ticks without modifying the scale.
      Specified by:
      calculateMinorTickMarks in class javafx.scene.chart.ValueAxis<Number>
      Returns:
      the location of tick marks on the natural scale.

    • calculateTickValues

      protected List<Number> calculateTickValues(double length, Object range)
      Calculate the major ticks without modifying the scale. This implementation gives ticks at every integer on the transformed scale. Consider overriding.
      Specified by:
      calculateTickValues in class javafx.scene.chart.Axis<Number>
      Parameters:
      length - The length of the axis in display units
      range - A range object returned from autoRange()
      Returns:
      the tick values on the natural scale.

    • getTickMarkLabel

      protected String getTickMarkLabel(Number value)
      Specified by:
      getTickMarkLabel in class javafx.scene.chart.Axis<Number>

    • autoRange

      protected Object autoRange(double minValue, double maxValue, double length, double labelSize)
      Create a range object to update the chart. Consider overriding.
      Overrides:
      autoRange in class javafx.scene.chart.ValueAxis<Number>
      Parameters:
      minValue - The min data value that needs to be plotted on this axis
      maxValue - The max data value that needs to be plotted on this axis
      length - The length of the axis in display coordinates
      labelSize - The approximate average size a label takes along the axis
      Returns:

    • setRange

      protected void setRange(Object range, boolean animate)
      Use the range returned by getRange() or autoRange(). Consider overriding.
      Specified by:
      setRange in class javafx.scene.chart.Axis<Number>
      Parameters:
      range - A range object returned from autoRange()
      animate - If true animate the change in range

    • getRange

      protected Object getRange()
      Query the current range. Consider overriding.
      Specified by:
      getRange in class javafx.scene.chart.Axis<Number>
      Returns:
      An arbitrary range object; often an array.
